items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has warned that credentials linked to Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways have been exposed, meaning login details for these common network security devices may be in the hands of attackers. If your organisation uses Fortinet products, IT teams should treat this as urgent. Separately, a Cloudflare report has identified rising cyberattacks targeting civil society organisations, including Australian ones — a reminder that no sector is too small to be targeted.

An IBM study also highlights that many executives are struggling to maintain control over how AI tools handle sensitive data — a growing concern for Australian RTOs managing student records.

**Practical tip:** If your organisation uses Fortinet firewalls or VPNs, ask your IT provider today whether your credentials have been reviewed and changed recently.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of widespread credential exposure affecting critical Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways nationwide.
